{{Quote|I saw you looking at the Yagaran aleudrupe plants there. You will see many such non-native plants in the jungle, I'm afraid — leavings of previous visitors to the Varonat jungle who were less than careful with their provisions.|Falmal to Talon Karrde during their Morodin hunting expedition|<ref name="FC" />}}
The '''Yagaran aleudrupe plant''' was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Plant/Legends|plant]] with pink shoots and that grew edible [[Aleudrupe berry/Legends|berries]] which were considered a delicacy on many [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. [[Gamgalon]] used them and two other legal chemicals in order to make a gas that could be used much like [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna]] gas.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Aleudrupe berries are considered a delicacy on many worlds.|Falmal to Talon Karrde|<ref name="FC" />}}
The Yagaran aleudrupe plant was grown in soil and had [[Color/Legends|pink]]-colored shoots. The plant produced a [[Aleudrupe berry/Legends|berry]] that had pips inside which were used to propagate the species.<ref name="FC" />

==History==
Originating from the [[Yagara system/Legends|Yagara system]],<ref name="Naming" /> the berries of the Yagaran aleudrupe plant were considered to be a delicacy in many [[Star system/Legends|systems]]. The aleudrupe plant was introduced to the [[Great Jungle]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] on the [[Ison Trade Corridor/Legends|Ison Corridor]] sometime before [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. The [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Gamgalon]] found that when the soil the plant was growing in was covered by the slime trail of the native [[Morodin/Legends|Morodin]], the pips of the plant's berries underwent a chemical change. These altered pips, when combined with [[rethan-K]] and [[promhassic triaxli]], acted as a catalyst to create a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] formulation as powered as spin-sealed [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna]] gas. All three components were perfectly legal and did not raise flags with customs officials, allowing them to be shipped to planets without arousing suspicions.<ref name="FC" />

Gamgalon began growing the plants along the Morodin' slime trailss, marking the positions of the plantings with [[transpond marker]]s for later harvesting. To cover their operation in Varonat's Great Jungle, Gamgalon established a safari hunting the Morodin, and bribed the local [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Ison Corridor)|Imperial governor]] to turn a blind eye to his operations. The [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Talon Karrde]] uncovered Gamgalon's activities in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], and during his encounter with the Krish, Gamgalon ended up dead,<ref name="FC" /> and Karrde left the operation in ruins.<ref name="GG-Hoth">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040325web|text=Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin|int=Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Yagaran aleudrupe plant first appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s ''[[First Contact]]'', which was published in [[1994]]'s [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] by [[West End Games]]. ''First Contact'' was subsequently reprinted in ''[[Tales from the Empire]]''. ''[[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4]]'', and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']].
